mnemonics: Every Sequence Converges Inside (ESCI): In a sequentially compact metric space, every sequence has a subsequence that converges INSIDE the space.
keyPoints: A metric space (X, d) is sequentially compact if every sequence in X has a convergent subsequence whose limit is also in X.
In metric spaces, sequential compactness is equivalent to the topological definition of compactness (every open cover has a finite subcover).
The Bolzano-Weierstrass Theorem states that a subset of R^n is sequentially compact if and only if it is closed and bounded.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that the limit of the convergent subsequence must lie within the original space X, not just in some larger space.
Confusing sequential compactness with total boundedness; a space can be totally bounded but not complete (and therefore not sequentially compact).
Assuming that a closed and bounded set is compact in every metric space, which is only true in R^n (by the Heine-Borel Theorem) and not in general.
